items.title: NOK: Norges Bank hawkish shift supports currency – ING
items.summary: ING’s Francesco Pesole notes that Norges Bank delivered a hawkish shift, signalling upcoming rate hikes as inflation concerns extend beyond the energy shock. The bank now fully prices at least one hike in its projections, while ING expects only one move given its bearish Oil and gas baseline.

items.title: Oil: Volatile price action on ceasefire uncertainty – ING
items.summary: ING’s Ewa Manthey and Warren Patterson note that NYMEX WTI and ICE Brent are up around 2% after sharp volatility around $100/bbl as markets react to conflicting US‑Iran ceasefire signals.

items.title: Norges Bank: Higher rate path supports stronger Krone – Nordea
items.summary: Nordea's Chief economist Kjetil Olsen and Senior Macro and FX Strategist Sara Midtgaard note that the central bank of Norway, Norges Bank now signals a policy rate hike to 4.25% at an upcoming meeting, with the new path pointing to 4.25–4.50% by year-end.

items.title: GBP: Resilience faces fiscal and growth risks – OCBC
items.summary: OCBC strategists Sim Moh Siong and Christopher Wong highlight that the Pound (GBP) has been resilient since the Iran conflict, supported by a sharp hawkish shift in UK rate expectations.

items.title: MXN: Banxico patience supports peso – Commerzbank
items.summary: Commerzbank analysts Michael Pfister and Norman Liebke report that the Bank of Mexico (Banxico) is expected to leave rates unchanged, with markets no longer pricing cuts in the near term after the Iran conflict.

items.title: Reuters Poll: Fed to hold policy rate at 3.50%-3.75% until at least September
items.summary: 55 of 82 economists polled by Reuters think that the Federal Reserve (Fed) will keep the policy rate unchanged at the 3.5%-3.75% range at least until September.

items.title: NZD/USD extends losses below 0.5800 as hopes of a US-Iran peace deal ebb
items.summary: The New Zealand Dollar (NZD) heads south against the US Dollar (USD) for the third consecutive day, extending its reversal from last week's highs, a few pips short of 0.5900, to session lows at 0.5781 on Thursday’s European trading session.The risk-sensitive Kiwi has been hit by a sour market sentim

items.title: USD: War-driven bid and funding stress risks – BBH
items.summary: Brown Brothers Harriman’s (BBH) Elias Haddad notes renewed risk aversion is lifting the Dollar as the Iran conflict escalates, with Oil higher and global equities and bonds weaker.

items.title: ZAR: SARB holds through conflict – Commerzbank
items.summary: Commerzbank analyst Volkmar Baur expects the South African Reserve Bank (SARB) to keep rates unchanged, despite inflation having fallen to its new 3% target and real rates remaining restrictive.

items.title: USD: Real rate support capped by Europe – BNY
items.summary: BNY strategist Geoff Yu argues that rising European real yields are increasingly offsetting traditional support for the Dollar from higher US real rates. He notes that the spread between US and European real rates has stayed tight, limiting further Dollar upside.

items.title: NZD/USD Price Forecast: Slips below 0.5800 toward two-month lows
items.summary: NZD/USD extends its losing streak for the third successive day, trading around 0.5780 during the European hours on Thursday. The technical analysis of the daily chart signals a bearish bias as the pair is consolidating within a descending channel pattern.

items.title: Fed: Expected to stay on hold – ABN AMRO
items.summary: ABN AMRO’s Senior US Economist Rogier Quaedvlieg argues that US growth has slowed sharply outside AI-related activity, with near-zero job creation and downgraded GDP.
